summarization.a: The etch depth increases linearly with the acceleration grid voltage, after a threshold voltage has been reached.
summarization.b: The etch depth increases with CI species exposure time at both 15V and 10V acceleration voltages.

b.question: Is there a linear trend in Cl species exposure time and etch depth?
Why can a higher etch depth be achieved with larger acceleration voltage?
b.answer: b	Ions gain more directional energy towards the substrate.

a.question: Why is it likely that there is a threshold voltage afterwhich the etch depth increases?
a.answer: a	The acceleration voltage is related to the energy with which ions bombard the surface. If this energy is too low, there will not be any etching.

a.question: If the data point at 10 V would have been at a different Cl species exposure time, would the etch depth be higher or lower?
a.answer: a	For longer exposure time it could be that due to the higher ion dose there would be some etching. Lower exposure time would however result in the same value, 0.
